-- 1 of 4 --

The Seed Launch Part 4
!
Next up with the seed launch is the Hyper Seed Launch. This is really powerful stuff.
It comes with a few warnings, but this is crazy powerful. It can be used to create a
business, a serious business, out of thin air. It's not necessarily easy, but I've seen it
done several times very successfully – major league businesses. One of them is multi-
million dollar, in fact I think it's approaching eight figures, which is $10 million a year. It
was started using this method.
!
This is powerful stuff. Now I do have to give you the shenanigans warning. This could
be abused and I've seen it used a couple times where I was not comfortable with the
way it was used. I'll get more into that a little bit later. You have to use this one
ethically. Don't be a slimeball. The other thing is it's not necessarily the easiest or the
simplest thing. Well, it actually is pretty simple, not necessarily super easy, but it's very
doable. I've seen it done.
!
There has to be some chutzpah or some gumption. You need some deal-making. You
need to able to talk to people. You need to be able to do a little bit of juggling to make
this happen. If you can do that, this is really cool stuff. I've actually never taught this
before.
!
First of all, this is all about leveraging experts and basically going out and
interviewing experts and tapping into their resources. This is the thing a lot of people
don't understand. In most markets, it's not hard to find experts that will agree to be
interviewed.
!
The easiest is generally book authors. People that have published books are usually
easy to line up to interview them. A lot of us look up to book authors, we think they
have their book on Amazon, they have their hardcover or softcover book out there, and
they're amazing celebrities.
!
The reality is that it doesn't take too much to get a book published. It takes a lot to sell
a book, so most book authors, they have one thing that they think about 24 hours a
day. That is how to sell more books. Either that, or if it's been a while since they
published their book they feel bitter that they didn't sell more books. They have a
bunch of books sitting around or they've never earned back on their royalties, so
they've never gotten paid anything beyond their initial advance. Most authors are just
desperate to get some promotion.
!
Interviews give them some promotion, a potential to sell some more books. One of the
things you can do is just go to Amazon and do a search on whatever your topic is. If it's
growing tomatoes, if you go and you type in gardening or growing vegetables, you'll get
a whole list of books and you just look down the list and find 20 books. Find the
authors' contact information, most of them have websites. Find the authors' contact
information, contact them, email them. Most of them are going to be happy to be
interviewed.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2

-- 2 of 4 --

!
Someone is only an expert if they have an audience and if you have an audience or the
potential of an audience, they're interested in talking to you. Most experts in most
markets really aren't making much money at all. There are exceptions, like internet
marketing. I get asked to be interviewed all the time. I just don't have time to say yes
to all the requests.
!
I've done plenty of these myself, where I've been interviewed. An area like internet
marketing, to get the people at the top of the heap to say yes to something like this is a
little harder. There are plenty of what we call the Tier B people, maybe not the highest
or the most renowned folks, but you can always find lesser-known people.
!
It's easy to get to find experts to interview. All experts want to sell more product. The
other thing is they might not even have a product. If you do an interview with them
and agree to give them the interview, often that's a great hook to get someone to
agree to do this. One thing you want to do, actually I'm going to talk about this a little
bit later, the legalities of this.
!
Generally, if someone agrees to be interviewed, to tell you the truth in most markets
there's not much paperwork going back and forth. It's implied agreement. If you tell
them what you're doing with the interview, there usually is not going to be any legal
issues.
!
If you wanted to, you could do a work-for-hire agreement or do a release, where
they're releasing the rights to the interview. The reality is, in most markets, that doesn't
really happen. It's more done on a handshake basis.
!
I'm not saying that's the way you would necessarily want to do it, but that is the way it
is done in most markets.
!
There are two main ways to do this. One I'm calling the Product Build-out. In the
Product Build-out, the way you do this is you interview the experts, you record the
interviews, you get the interviews transcribed. Then you go back and you give the
experts a copy of the recording and transcripts. That is just sort of playing nice. Then
you package the series of interviews into a product and then you do a product launch.
!
Say you've interviewed six experts and now you have six audios. Maybe you interviewed
them for 40-60 minutes each and now you have a product. It's six hours with six
experts and if you wanted to you could put an intro, get it transcribed, then you could
add your notes in, your notes for each interview, you could put on an introduction video
or audio and a conclusion video or audio and build out a very nice product.
!
The real effort here is you've gone and identified the experts and you've interviewed
them. It doesn't take much work. Typically if you have 12-15 questions that will more
than take up an hour. In fact, most of the time six or eight questions will take up an
hour, if you have a good expert.

-- 3 of 4 --

!
You just come up with a number of questions. You could go to your list, no matter how
big or small, and tell them you're going to be doing these interviews and ask them what
they would like you to ask the expert.
!
The way I've got this laid out, you're actually doing these calls and these interviews
with the expert and you're recording them ahead of time. You're not doing them as
teleseminars or webinars. It is just generally much less stress that way.
If the expert knows that it's just a recorded interview, they're not doing it in front of a
live audience, then it usually relaxes them. You can always go back and make changes
and edit them.
!
Alternatively, you could do them as teleseminars or even webinars. It's your choice, but
it's usually easier to just record them ahead of time. There are lots of different ways to
do this.
!
I've actually recently recorded them on Skype. The best is, not to get too technical, but
if you do it on Skype and you both record your side of the conversation. Then you
basically put the two of them together in some audio-editing software. I don't want to
get too in-depth into that.
!
Another way you can do this is just get a bridge line, which is what you would get for a
teleseminar, but it's just the two of you on it, but most of them have a record function.
You can record that way.
!
Alternatively you can get together, if you live in the same city, you could just sit down
with each other and record it using any type of recording device.
!
What happens, though, is after you do this, you put the product together, then you go
back and you ask the experts to promote the launch as affiliates. Then you would do
what is basically in between a JV launch and an internal launch. Basically, it's not
guaranteed they're going to promote, but it makes them more predisposed to
promoting if they're actually in the product.
!
They promote your launch, they help build your list, and you're paying them affiliate
commission, so they're making some money. And you have yourself a product and a list
and a product launch. This can be super effective.
!
